findings.title: Generalization experiment (Table 4 / Fig 3) random half-station split not in repo
findings.quote: We randomly sample half of the stations from the year 2017-2022/2023 for training and validation, while using the remaining stations from 2024 as the test set.
findings.claim: The paper's headline generalization result (Table 4, Fig 3, and a third contribution bullet in the intro) is produced by randomly partitioning stations into two disjoint halves and training on one half (2017-2023) while testing on the unseen half (2024). The shipped code's only split is by year (train_years 2017-2022, val 2023, test 2024 in configs/hgnn_gcn_edge.yaml); no script samples or holds out half the stations, and a static scan of all core .py files (see check script) finds no station-sampling / disjoint-half logic.
findings.concern: A central claim of the paper (generalization to unseen stations) cannot be reproduced because the procedure that creates the unseen-station split is absent from the repository.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the script that builds the random half-station train/test partition for the generalization experiment, including the seed used, so Table 4 and Fig 3 can be reproduced.
findings.paper_ref: Section 4.3 Global Generalization Analysis; Table 4; Figure 3

findings.title: Persistence baseline reported in every results table has no code
findings.claim: A 'Persistence' baseline is reported in Tables 1, 2 and 4 and Figures 4 and 6, but no code computes it: a case-insensitive scan of all core .py files (see check script) finds no 'persistence' / last-value predictor, and model_st.py's model registry (lines 41-85) contains no persistence entry.
findings.concern: The naive lower-bound baseline cannot be reproduced from the repo, and a reader cannot verify the Persistence numbers that anchor task difficulty.
findings.resolution: Authors: please provide the script that computes the Persistence (last-day-carried-forward) baseline used in Tables 1/2/4 and Figs 4/6.
findings.paper_ref: Table 1 'Persistence' row; Table 2; Table 4; Figures 4 and 6

findings.title: Per-variable baseline hyperparameter tuning (Table 7) has no tuning code
findings.quote: For a fair comparison, we tune different hyperparameters for all baselines, finding the best setting for each.
findings.claim: The paper reports per-variable optimal learning rate / batch size / hidden size for all 13 baselines in Table 7 and states each baseline was tuned, but the repo contains no tuning/sweep driver for the core spatiotemporal baselines: configs/spatial_temporal.yaml hard-codes a single hidden_size/lr, and a scan of core .py files finds no sweep/optuna/ray/hyperopt harness (the only 'tune' matches are the literal filename 'unique_primary_station_ids_<var>_tune.csv').
findings.concern: Without the tuning harness and search ranges, the fairness of the baseline comparison (whether baselines were tuned with the same budget as MIGN) cannot be verified or reproduced.
findings.resolution: Authors: please provide the sweep configuration / search ranges and the script used to select the Table 7 baseline hyperparameters.

findings.title: No requirements/environment file; core deps only loosely pinned in README prose
findings.quote:
# Install PyTorch (CUDA 12.1)
pip install torch==2.1.0 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121

# Install DGL (CUDA 12.1 compatible)
pip install dgl -f https://data.dgl.ai/wheels/torch-2.1/cu121/repo.html

# Install additional dependencies
pip install numpy==1.26.0
pip install healpy
pip install lightning
pip install -U 'wandb>=0.12.10'
pip install jupyter
findings.claim: There is no requirements.txt / environment.yml for the core MIGN code; dependencies are listed only as README pip commands, and several packages the code imports (e.g. lightning, healpy, einops, thop, torchstat, torch_geometric, tsl) are unpinned or unlisted (torchstat/thop/einops/tsl appear in imports but not in the README install list).
findings.concern: The exact environment cannot be rebuilt deterministically, which can change numerical results and block reproduction.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add a pinned requirements.txt (or environment.yml) covering all imported packages including einops, thop, torchstat, torch_geometric, torch-spatiotemporal.

findings.title: Data dirs and run commands hardcode the authors' machine paths
findings.claim: The data/embedding/climatology directories are hardcoded to the authors' absolute paths (`/data/zzn/insitu/data`) in both configs/hgnn_gcn_edge.yaml and configs/spatial_temporal.yaml, and the README run commands invoke `python /home/zinanzheng/project/MIGN/main.py ...`; there is no CLI flag or env var to override the data root.
findings.concern: Out of the box the code cannot locate the downloaded data on any other machine; a user must hand-edit multiple config files, and step0-step3 scripts similarly hardcode `/data/zzn/insitu/data` and `/mnt/hda/zzn/realtime/Dataset`.
findings.resolution: Authors: parameterize the data root (CLI flag / env var) instead of hardcoding it in the YAML configs and the README commands.

findings.title: Hugging Face 'processed data' provides only DEWP, not all 6 reported variables
findings.quote:
## Processed Data

We provide **processed datasets** for both **baseline models** and **MIGN** at  
- **spherical level 3**  
- **HEALPix level 3**

These datasets are designed for **one-step input → one-step output** prediction tasks.

You can directly download the processed data from our Hugging Face repository:  
👉 [compasszzn/MIGN](https://huggingface.co/datasets/compasszzn/MIGN)
findings.claim: The README presents the Hugging Face repo as the ready-to-use processed dataset, but the HF repo (retrieved 2026-05-30) contains only `pyg_DEWP_20_graph_step_1.zip`, `dgl_neighbor_10_step_1_refine_3.zip`, `DEWP_embeddings_3.pt`, and `3_healpix_embeddings_level_3.pt` — i.e. processed PyG baseline data and SH station-embeddings only for the DEWP variable, whereas the paper reports 6 variables (MAX/MIN/DEWP/SLP/WDSP/MXSPD).
findings.concern: Reproducing the baseline tables for the other five variables requires re-running the full raw-data pipeline (download + step0-step4), not just the advertised download; the 'safely skip Data Preparation' note holds only for DEWP.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm whether processed PyG data and SH embeddings for the remaining five variables are intended to be on Hugging Face, or document that step4 must be re-run per variable.

findings.title: Normalization mean/std fit over all years 2017-2024 including val and test
findings.claim: step2_climatology.py globs ALL `*.csv` in each variable folder with no year filter and takes np.nanmean/np.nanstd over the whole matrix. step0_filter_feature.py (loop `range(2017, 2025)`) writes every year 2017-2024 into that same folder, so the per-variable mean/std used to z-normalize inputs/labels (dataset.py:46,115,169,250) are computed over the validation (2023) and test (2024) years as well as train.
findings.concern: The normalization statistics see the test distribution (a form of preprocessing-before-split leakage); because it is a single global scalar mean/std per variable the practical effect is small, but it technically violates the train-only-statistics principle and applies identically to MIGN and all baselines.
findings.resolution: Authors: recompute climatology using only the 2017-2022 training files and confirm Table 1/2 numbers are unchanged, or clarify that the leak is negligible.
